MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Dec-09-2025 Continued To Sep-14-26 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-11-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Daniel Feder, Esq. 415-710-3277 for Plaintiff.
Amy Todd, Esq. (In-person) Kevin Hambly, Esq. 619-881-4485 Jackson Lewis P.C. for Defendant.
Court reporter: Jane Hong, CSR #11975, 909-831-8819, csrjane@hotmail.com

Parties state appearance. The Court announces the tentative ruling. The Court states a timely notice of contest the TR was received.

ARGUED.

Amy Todd contests and presents arguments; John Doe designation be stricken, highly prejudicial, Plaintiff allowed to hide, cites paragraph 22-23 of the complaint, case authority not aligned with this case, set a dangerous precedent, no issue of retaliation, cot current employess.

Court comments; nature of allegation, don't do this often, grant 3% of pseudonym orders, pretty mindful of public's right to access, protective order or sealing more cumbersome, facts matter, Defense brought issue to the court's attention.

Mr. Feder responds to counsel's arguments; aware of the rule and had a draft, sexual assault case, sensitive psychological conditions, irreversible harms, immigration consequences, Salacious allegations, no prejudcie to Defense, jury instruction on disclosure, motion tactical, cites paragraph 23 of complaint.

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

Defendant's motion to strike.

Taken Under Submission.***

(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court reporter: Jane Hong, CSR #11975, 909-831-8819, csrjane@hotmail.com, Reported. (302/JMQ)

***After the hearing, the court signs an order. (see scanned order for details)***

Off Calendar Mar-19-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Daniel Feder, Esq. 415-391-9476 Law Office for Plaintiff. (In-person)
Kevin Hambly, Esq. 619-573-4900 Jackson Lewis for Defendant.

Parties state appearance. The Court announces the tentative ruling. The Court states a timely notice of contest the TR was received.

ARGUED. Mr. Hambly agrees with the TR. Mr. Feder presents arguments; states client was deposed and contests sanctions. The Court orders counsel to meet and confer re: continuation of the deposition.

After arguments, the Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

Defendants Zing Labs and Lawrence Goldstein's Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Deposition And For Reasonable Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $15,238.00.

The Court orders counsel to meet and confer re: continuation of the deposition. The matter is taken under submission.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
